/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Shared/Windows.commit_message: TLAB-929 updated include files for windows syslog snare parser
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Shared/Windows.description: This parser defines agents headers that send Windows text based logs via syslog
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Shared/Windows.name: Windows Forwarding Headers
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Shared/Windows.parser_text:
# =========================================================================================
# Read Me!
# =========================================================================================

# Please standardize all parsed key names as much as possible across the various agents with their own Windows header data! 
# The CSE event ID is created at the end of the main transform using "win_header_channel" and "win_header_event_id"
# win_header_channel - The channel the event came from. Basically the event log. Make sure it is the channel name and not the provider name as they are similar, but different. For example:
	# Channel Name: "Security" Provider Name: "Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing"
    # Channel Name: "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" Provider Name: "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
    # Channel Name: "Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational" Provider Name: "Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell"
# win_header_event_id - The numeric event ID code for the log event from the event log aka win_header_channel
# event_log_text - The rest of the text from the event log once the header ends
# Look at the other created Windows headers in this file for additional similar names to use for new headers

# Windows Event Header Schema Reference
# https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/wes/eventschema-elements

# Also, be sure to include both START_TIME_FIELD and TIME_PARSER to accommodate time parsing after your regex
# https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/format/DateTimeFormatter.html

# =========================================================================================
# Main Transform
# =========================================================================================
[transform:Parse_Windows_Text_Header]
TRANSFORM_CASCADE:_$log_entry = Tenable_LCE,Snare

# Set CSE Configuration Information
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{win_header_channel}}-{{win_header_event_id}}

# =========================================================================================
# Tenable Log Correlation Engine (LCE)
# =========================================================================================
[transform:Tenable_LCE]
# Security,11/16/2020,09:34:36 AM,Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing,4688,Information,Success Audit,Process Creation,-,computer.domain.local,IP:10.198.96.29,4720,
# Sometimes the header will start with the unicode character 'ZERO WIDTH NO-BREAK SPACE' (FEFF)
# https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/feff/index.htm

REGEX = .*\b(?<win_header_channel>[a-zA-Z\-\/]+),(?<win_header_date>\d{2}\/\d{2}\/\d{4}),(?<win_header_time>\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\s\w{2}),(?<win_header_provider>.+),(?<win_header_event_id>\d+),(?<win_header_severity>\w+),(?<win_header_event_type>.+),(?<win_header_event_category>.+),(?<win_header_entity>.+),(?<win_header_computer_name>.+),IP:(?<win_header_computer_ip>[\d.]+),\d+,(?<event_log_text>.+)

SET:combine_date_and_time = {{win_header_date}} {{win_header_time}}
START_TIME_FIELD = combine_date_and_time
TIME_PARSER = MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss a

# =========================================================================================
# Snare for Windows
# =========================================================================================
[transform:Snare]
# MSWinEventLog	1	Security	169231760	Tue Mar 30 13:19:24 2021	4624	Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing	N/A	N/A	Success Audit	computer.domain.local	Logon		
# The fields are tab separated and the Snare header ends with two tabs. The message also ends with a final tab.

REGEX = .*MSWinEventLog\s+(?<win_header_severity>(\d+|-))\s+(?<win_header_channel>[^\t|^\s]+)\t(?<win_header_snare_event_counter>(\d+|-))\t(?<win_header_timestamp>.+)\t(?<win_header_event_id>(\d+|-))\t(?<win_header_provider>[^\t|^\s]+)\t(?<win_header_username>[^\t|^\s]+)\t(?<win_header_sid>[^\t|^\s]+)\t(?<win_header_event_type>(Failure Audit|Information|Success Audit|-))\t(?<win_header_computer_name>[^\t|^\s]+)\t(?<win_header_event_category>[^\t]+)\t+(?<event_log_text>.+?) *(?:\t(-)?\d+(\\r\\n)?$|\sThs\sevent\s|\sThis\sevent\s|\s*Token\sElevation\sType\sindicates|\sCertificate\sinformation\sis\s|\sTicket\soptions\sand\s)

# We have to strip the day of the week off of the timestamp in the snare header in order for the time parser to work.
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:win_header_timestamp=Snare_Transform_Timestamp

[transform:Snare_Transform_Timestamp]
REGEX=\w{1,3}\s+(?<Transformed_timestamp>.*)
START_TIME_FIELD = Transformed_timestamp
TIME_PARSER =MMM dd HH:mm:ss yyyy
DROP:Transformed_timestamp=true

REGEX = .*

[transform:Transform_Security_1100]
REGEX = (?<description>.*)

[transform:Transform_Security_1102]
TRANSFORM_CASCADE = Transform_AD_FS_1102, Security_1102

[transform:Transform_AD_FS_1102]
REGEX = .*Activity ID:\s*(?<EventData.ActivityID>.*?)\s*Request Details:\s*Client IP:\s*(?<EventData.ClientIP>.*?)\s*Requested URI:\s*(?<EventData.RequestedURI>.*?)\s*Additional details:\s*(?<EventData.AdditionalDetails>.*?)$
MAPPER:event_id = AD_FS-{{win_header_event_id}}

[transform:Security_1102]
REGEX = .*

[transform:Transform_Security_4610]
REGEX=.*Authentication Package Name: (?<EventData.AuthenticationPackageName>.*?)$

[transform:Transform_Security_4611]
REGEX=.*Subject:\s*Security ID:\s*(?<EventData.SubjectUserSid>.*?)\s*Account Name:\s*(?<EventData.SubjectUserName>.*?)\s*Account Domain:\s*(?<EventData.SubjectDomainName>.*?)\s*Logon ID:\s*(?<EventData.SubjectLogonId>.*?)\s*Logon Process Name:\s*(?<EventData.LogonProcessName>.*?)$

[transform:Transform_Security_4614]
REGEX=.*Notification Package Name: (?<EventData.NotificationPackageName>.*?)$

[transform:Transform_Security_4618]
REGEX=.*Subject:\s*Security ID:\s*(?<EventData.TargetUserSid>.*?)\s*Account Name:\s*(?<EventData.TargetUserName>.*?)\s*Account Domain:\s*(?<EventData.TargetUserDomain>.*?)\s*Logon ID:\s*(?<EventData.TargetLogonId>.*?)\s*Alert Information:\s*Computer:\s*(?<EventData.ComputerName>.*?)\s*Event ID:\s*(?<EventId>.*?)\s*Number of Events:\s*(?<EventCount>.*?)\s*Duration:\s*(?<EventData.Duration>.*?)\s*This event is generated when Windows is configured to generate alerts in accordance with the Common Criteria Security Audit Analysis requirements \(FAU_SAA\) and an auditable event pattern occurs\.

[transform:Transform_Security_4622]
REGEX=.*Security Package Name:\s*(?<EvenetData.SecurityPackageName>.*?)$

[transform:Transform_Security_4624]
TRANSFORM_CASCADE = Transform_Security_4624_Win_2008,Transform_Security_4624_Win_2012,Transform_Security_4624_Win_2016

[transform:Transform_Security_4624_Win_2008]

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Sysmon-JSON.commit_message: TLAB-1222 More robust time parsing for Windows
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Sysmon-JSON.description: Parser for Sysmon logs sent in JSON format. Sysmon logs in XML format should be handled with the Windows-XML parser.
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Sysmon-JSON.name: Sysmon-JSON
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Sysmon-JSON.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

#Establish the mapper config globally before passing to transforms to avoid having to define a mapper for every possible EventID
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{Channel}}-{{EventID}}

#Handle time
TRANSFORM=Time-Parser

#Transform logic to look for file hash fields since only some sysmon logs have them
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.Hashes = parse_sysmon_hashes
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.Hash = parse_sysmon_hashes
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.ConfigurationFileHash = parse_sysmon_hashes

#Regex to handle file hash fields that are formatted as '<hash_type>=<hash_value>' and are comma separated if multiple hash types exist
[transform:parse_sysmon_hashes]
REGEX = (?<_$FIELD_1>[^=]+)=(?<_$VAL_1>\w+),?
REPEAT_MATCH = TRUE

[transform:Time-Parser]
START_TIME_FIELD = TimeCreated
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows DNS.description: Parser for Windows Active Directory DNS logs sent via syslog in standard format.
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows DNS.name: Windows DNS
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows DNS.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT=REGEX

MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = DNS
MAPPER:event_id = DNS

TRANSFORM = decode_msg
FIND_REPLACE:questionname:. = \(\d+\)
FIND_REPLACE:questionname: = ^\.|\.$
RENAME_FIELD:domain = questionname
TRANSFORM:flags = decode_flags

SET:timestamp = {{date}} {{time}}
START_TIME_FIELD = timestamp
TIME_PARSER = MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss a,M/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss a,dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss,d/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss

#Drop intermediate processing fields
DROP:flags = true

[transform:decode_msg]
REGEX = ^(?<date>\d\S+)\s+(?<time>\d\S+(?:\s[AP]M)?)\s+(?<threadid>\d\S+)\s+(?<context>\S+)\s+(?<ici>\S+)\s+(?<tcpudp>\S+)\s+(?<sndrcv>\S+)\s+(?<remoteip>\S+)\s+(?<xid>\S+)\s((?<rq>.)\s)?(?<opcode>.)\s+\[(?<flags>.*?)\]\s+(?<questiontype>\S+)\s+(?<questionname>.*)$

[transform:decode_flags]
REGEX = ^(?P<hex>\S+)\s+((?P<code>.{1,4})\s+)?(?P<responsecode>\S+)$

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Defend.description: Parser for Windows Defender ATP Alert logs sent via Azure API
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Defend.name: Windows Defender ATP Alert JSON
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Defend.parser_text:
###################################
#            Resources            #
###################################
#https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/connectors/wdatp/#alert

[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

#Set mapper information
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{detectionSource}}

JSON_DROP_NULLS = true

#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = _messagetime
TIME_PARSER = X1000

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Defend.name: Windows Defender SCCM DB CSV
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Defend.parser_text:
# For Windows Defender (System Center Endpoint Protection) logs collected from SCCM DB via PowerShell
# https://gist.github.com/Ioan-Popovici/ffa7faecbeefc0ccdf4ae6aa99957fe8

[parser]
# Establish mapper configuration for all events in one place
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = Alert

# Establish log format and fields
FORMAT = CSV
EVENT_MULTILINE = False
FIELDS = "Type","RowID","Name","Description","Timestamp","SchemaVersion","ObserverHost","ObserverUser","ObserverProductName","ObserverProductversion","ObserverProtectionType","ObserverProtectionVersion","ObserverProtectionSignatureVersion","ObserverDetection","ObserverDetectionTime","ActorHost","ActorUser","ActorProcess","ActorResource","ActionType","TargetHost","TargetUser","TargetProcess","TargetResource","ClassificationID","ClassificationType","ClassificationSeverity","ClassificationCategory","RemediationType","RemediationResult","RemediationErrorCode","RemediationPendingAction","IsActiveMalware"

#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = Timestamp
TIME_PARSER = MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss a, M/dd/yyyy h:mm:ss a

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Firewa.name: Windows Firewall Syslog
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows Firewa.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Syslog Headers Microsoft = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Forwarding Headers = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Text Transforms - Security = true

[parser]
# Parse Windows Forwarding Header
TRANSFORM:_$log_entry = Parse_Windows_Text_Header

MAPPER:event_id = {{win_header_provider}}-{{win_header_channel}}-{{win_header_event_id}}

TRANSFORM_CASCADE:event_log_text = transform1,transform2,transform3,transform4,transform5,transform6,transform7

DROP:event_log_text = true

[transform:transform1]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\.\s+(?:Added|Modified|Deleted)\s+Rule:(?P<rule>.*?)\s+Rule\s+ID:\s+(?P<rule_id>.*?)\s+Rule\s+Name:\s+(?P<rule_name>.*?)(?:\s+Origin:\s+(?P<origin>.*?))?(?:\s+Active:\s+(?P<active>.*?))?(?:\s+Direction:\s+(?P<direction>.*?))?(?:\s+Profiles:\s+(?P<profiles>.*?))?(?:\s+Action:\s+(?P<action>.*?))?(?:\s+Application\s+Path:\s+(?P<application_path>.*?))?(?:\s+Service\s+Name:\s+(?P<service_name>.*?))?(?:\s+Protocol:\s+(?P<protocol>.*?))?(?:\s+Security\s+Options:\s+(?P<security_options>.*?))?(?:\s+Edge\s+Traversal:\s+(?P<edge_traversal>.*?))?(?:\s+Modifying\s+User:\s+(?P<modifying_user>.*?))?\s+Modifying\s+Application:\s+(?<modifying_application>.*?)$

[transform:transform2]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\.\s+New\s+Setting:(?P<new_setting>.*?)(?:\s+Type:\s+(?P<type>.*?))?(?:\s+Inbound\s+Action\s+Value:\s+(?P<inbound_action_value>.*?))?(?:\s+Modifying\s+User:\s+(?P<modifying_user>.*?))?\s+Modifying\s+Application:\s+(?P<modifying_application>.*?)$

[transform:transform3]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\.\s+IPsec\s+Phase:\s+(?P<ipsec_phase>.*?)(?:\s+Store\s+Type:\s+(?P<store_type>.*?))?(?:\s+ModifyingUser:\s+(?P<modifying_user>.*?))?\s+ModifyingApplication:\s+(?P<modifying_application>.*?)$

[transform:transform4]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\,\s+preClassifyEnabled:\s+(?P<preClassifyEnabled>.*?)(?:\s+policyChanged:\s+(?P<policyChanged>.*?))?(?:\s+activeProfiles:\s+(?P<activeProfiles>.*?))?\s+totalTimeMs:\s+(?P<totalTimeMs>.*?)\s+dataUpdated:\s+(?:")?(?P<dataUpdated>.*?)(?:")?$

[transform:transform5]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\.\s+Adapter\s+GUID:\s+(?P<adapter_gUID>.*?)(?:\s+Adapter\s+Name:\s+(?P<adapter_name>.*?))?(?:\s+Old\s+Profile:\s+(?P<old_profile>.*?))?(?:\s+New\s+Profile:\s+(?P<new_profile>.*?))?$

[transform:transform6]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\.\s+Reason:\s+(?P<reason>.*?)(?:\s+App\s+Path:\s+(?P<app_path>.*?))?(?:\s+IP\s+Version:(?P<ip_version>.*?))?(?:\s+IPv4\s+Protocol:(?P<ipv4_protocol>.*?))?(?:\s+(?:TCP|UDP)\s+Port:\s+(?P<port>.*?))?(?:\s+Process\s+ID:\s+(?P<process_id>.*?))?(?:\s+User:\s+(?P<user>.*?))?$

[transform:transform7]
REGEX = ^(?P<description>.*?)\.\s+(?P<action>.*?)$

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows PowerS.commit_message: Fix for alt format powershell logs
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows PowerS.name: Windows PowerShell-JSON
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows PowerS.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT=JSON

RENAME_FIELD:Channel = channel
RENAME_FIELD:EventID = event_id.id
RENAME_FIELD:TimeCreated = system_time

TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:EventData.ContextInfo = extract_context_info
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:event_data.data.1. = extract_event_data1
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:event_data.data.2. = extract_event_data2
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:event_data.data.3. = extract_event_data3
DROP:context_info = true
DROP:EventData.ContextInfo = true
DROP:Message = true
DROP:message = true
DROP:event_data.data.1. = true
DROP:event_data.data.2. = true
DROP:event_data.data.3. = true

MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:Product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{Channel}}-{{EventID}}

START_TIME_FIELD = TimeCreated
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX

[transform:extract_context_info]
REGEX = .*ContextInfo":\s*"(?<context_info>[^"]+).*
FIND_REPLACE:context_info:## = \\r\\n\s*
TRANSFORM:context_info = split_fields

[transform:extract_event_data1]
FIND_REPLACE:event_data.data.1.:##$1\= = \s+(\S+)=
TRANSFORM:event_data.data.1. = split_fields

[transform:extract_event_data2]
FIND_REPLACE:event_data.data.2.:##$1\= = \s+(\S+)=
TRANSFORM:event_data.data.2. = split_fields

[transform:extract_event_data3]
FIND_REPLACE:event_data.data.3.:##$1\= = \s+(\S+)=
TRANSFORM:event_data.data.3. = split_fields

[transform:split_fields]
REGEX = (\#\#)?(?<_$FIELD_1>[^=]+)=(?<_$VAL_1>[^\#]+)
REPEAT_MATCH=TRUE

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows PowerS.name: Windows PowerShell-Syslog
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows PowerS.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Syslog Headers Microsoft = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Forwarding Headers = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Text Transforms - Security = true

[parser]
# Parse Windows Forwarding Header
TRANSFORM:_$log_entry = Parse_Windows_Text_Header

MAPPER:event_id = {{win_header_provider}}/{{win_header_channel}}-{{win_header_event_id}}

TRANSFORM_CASCADE:event_log_text = parse_4103_1,parse_4103_2,parse_4104_1,parse_4104_2,parse_4104_3,parse_4103_3,no_fields

DROP:event_log_text = true

[transform:parse_4103_3]
REGEX = .*Severity\s+=\s+(?P<severity>\S+)(?:\s+Host\s+Name\s+=\s+(?P<host_name>\S+))?(?:\s+Host\s+Version\s+=\s+(?P<host_version>\S+))?(?:\s+Host\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<host_id>\S+))?(?:\s+Host\s+Application\s+=\s+(?P<host_app>.*?))?(?:\s+Engine\s+Version\s+=\s+(?P<engine_version>\S+))(?:\s+Runspace\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<runspace_id>\S+))(?:\s+Pipeline\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<pipline_id>\S+))(?:\s+Command\s+Name\s+=\s+(?P<command_name>\S+))?(?:\s+Command\s+Type\s+=\s+(?P<command_type>\S+))?(?:\s+Script\s+Name\s+=\s+(?P<script_name>.*\.\w+?)\s+Command\s+Path\s+=)?(?:\s+Sequence\s+Number\s+=\s+(?P<sequence_number>\S+))?(?:\s+User\s+=\s+(?P<user>\S+))?(?:\s+Connected\s+User\s+=)?(?:\s+Shell\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<shell_id>\S+))?(?:\s+User\s+Data:(?P<user_data>.*))?

[transform:parse_4104_1]
REGEX = Creating Scriptblock text.*?:\s+(?P<command>.*?)\s+\|\s+(?:\S+\s+)?ScriptBlock\s+ID:\s+(?P<scriptblock_id>\S+)\s+Path:(?P<path>.*)

[transform:parse_4103_2]
REGEX = (?P<win_header_channel>[a-zA-Z\-\/]+),(?P<win_header_date>\d{2}\/\d{2}\/\d{4}),(?P<win_header_time>\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\s\w{2}),(?P<win_header_provider>.+),(?P<win_header_event_id>\d+),(?P<win_header_severity>\w+),(?P<win_header_event_type>.+),(?P<win_header_event_category>.+),(?P<win_header_entity>.+),(?P<computer>.+),IP:(?P<win_header_computer_ip>[\d.]+),\d+,(?:\s|\S.)+Severity = (?P<severity>\b\w+\b)\s+Host Name = (?P<host_name>.*)\s*Host Version = (?P<host_version>[^\s]+)\s+Host ID = (?P<host_id>[^\s]+)\s+Host Application = (?P<host_app>.*?)\s+Engine Version = (?P<engine_version>[^\s]+)\s+Runspace ID = (?P<runspace_id>[^\s]+)\s+Pipeline ID = (?P<pipeline_id>\d+)\s+Command Name =\s*(?:(?P<cmd_name>\b\w+\b-.*)?)\s*Command Type =\s*(?P<cmd_type>\b\w+\b)\s*Script Name =\s*(?:(?P<script_name>.+?)?)\s*Command Path =(?:\s+)?((?P<cmd_path>.*?)?)\s+Sequence Number = (?P<sequence_number>\d+)\s+User = ((?P<user>[^\s]+)?)(?:(\s+)?)(?:(?:Connected User =.*)?)(?:(\s+)?)Shell ID = (?P<shell_id>[^\s]+)\s*User Data:\s?(?:(?P<user_data>[^\s])?)

[transform:parse_4104_2]
REGEX = (?P<win_header_channel>[a-zA-Z\-\/]+),(?P<win_header_date>\d{2}\/\d{2}\/\d{4}),(?P<win_header_time>\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\s\w{2}),(?P<win_header_provider>.+),(?P<win_header_event_id>\d+),(?P<win_header_severity>\w+),(?P<win_header_event_type>.+),(?P<win_header_event_category>.+),(?P<win_header_entity>.+),(?P<win_header_computer_name>.+),IP:(?P<win_header_computer_ip>[\d.]+),\d+,Creating Scriptblock text \(\d of \d\): (?P<EventData.ScriptBlockText>(\s|\S)+)\s*ScriptBlock ID: (?P<scriptblock_id>[^\s]+)\s*(?:Path: (?P<EventData.Path>.*))?

[transform:parse_4104_3]
REGEX = Creating Scriptblock text.*?:\s+(?P<command>.*)

[transform:parse_4103_1]
REGEX = .*Severity\s+=\s+(?P<severity>\S+)(?:\s+Host\s+Name\s+=\s+(?P<host_name>\S+))?(?:\s+Host\s+Version\s+=\s+(?P<host_version>\S+))?(?:\s+Host\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<host_id>\S+))?(?:\s+Host\s+Application\s+=\s+(?P<host_app>.*?))?(?:\s+Engine\s+Version\s+=\s+(?P<engine_version>\S+))(?:\s+Runspace\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<runspace_id>\S+))(?:\s+Pipeline\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<pipline_id>\S+))(?:\s+Command\s+Name\s+=\s+(?P<command_name>\S+))?(?:\s+Command\s+Type\s+=\s+(?P<command_type>\S+))?(?:\s+Script\s+Name\s+=\s+(?P<script_name>\S+))?(?:\s+Command\s+Path\s+=(?P<command_path>))?(?:\s+Sequence\s+Number\s+=\s+(?P<sequence_number>\S+))?(?:\s+User\s+=\s+(?P<user>\S+))?(?:\s+Connected\s+User\s+=)?(?:\s+Shell\s+ID\s+=\s+(?P<shell_id>\S+))?(?:\s+User\s+Data:(?P<user_data>.*))?$

[transform:no_fields]
REGEX = .*

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows XML fr.commit_message: TLAB-1222 More robust time parsing for Windows
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows XML fr.description: Parser for Windows XML logs centralized from Microsoft Azure logs sent in JSON format
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows XML fr.name: Windows XML from Azure
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows XML fr.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT=JSON

#Establish the mapper config globally before passing to transforms to avoid having to define a mapper for every possible EventID
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{EventLog}}-{{EventID}}

FIND_REPLACE:EventData:Event = DataItem
DROP:ParameterXml = TRUE

#Handle time
TRANSFORM=Time-Parser

#Transform logic to look for file hash fields since only some sysmon logs have them
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.Hashes = parse_sysmon_hashes
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.Hash = parse_sysmon_hashes
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.ConfigurationFileHash = parse_sysmon_hashes

#take syslog_message and apply XML parsing
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData = parse_xml

[transform:parse_xml]
FORMAT=XML

PARSE_ALL_TAG = Event
IGNORE_DICTIONARY_TAG = Event

#combine the seperated XML field attributes and elements
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.1.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.1}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.2.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.2}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.3.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.3}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.4.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.4}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.5.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.5}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.6.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.6}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.7.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.7}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.8.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.8}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.9.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.9}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.10.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.10}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.11.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.11}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.12.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.12}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.13.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.13}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.14.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.14}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.15.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.15}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.16.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.16}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.17.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.17}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.18.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.18}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.19.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.19}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.20.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.20}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.21.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.21}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.22.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.22}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.23.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.23}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.24.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.24}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.25.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.25}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.26.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.26}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.27.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.27}}
SET:EventData.{{EventData.Data.28.Name}} = {{EventData.Data.28}}


#drop the redundant fields used in the combination
DROP:EventData.Data.1.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.2.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.3.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.4.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.5.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.6.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.7.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.8.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.9.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.10.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.11.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.12.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.13.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.14.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.15.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.16.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.17.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.18.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.19.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.20.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.21.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.22.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.23.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.24.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.25.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.26.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.27.Name = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.28.Name = TRUE

DROP:EventData.Data.1 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.2 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.3 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.4 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.5 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.6 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.7 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.8 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.9 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.10 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.11 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.12 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.13 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.14 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.15 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.16 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.17 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.18 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.19 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.20 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.21 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.22 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.23 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.24 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.25 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.26 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.27 = TRUE
DROP:EventData.Data.28 = TRUE

#Regex to handle file hash fields that are formatted as '<hash_type>=<hash_value>' and are comma separated if multiple hash types exist
[transform:parse_sysmon_hashes]
REGEX = ^(MD5=(?<md5>[^,]{32}))?,?(SHA256=(?<sha256>[^,]{64}))?,?(SHA1=(?<sha1>[^,]{40}))?,?(IMPHASH=(?<imphash>[^,]{32,64}))?
SET:file_hash_sha256 = {{sha256}}
SET:file_hash_md5 = {{md5}}
SET:file_hash_sha1 = {{sha1}}
SET:file_hash_imphash = {{imphash}}
DROP:md5 = TRUE
DROP:sha256 = TRUE
DROP:sha1 = TRUE
DROP:imphash = TRUE

[transform:Time-Parser]
START_TIME_FIELD = TimeGenerated
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX

TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:RenderedDescription = parse_message
DROP:RenderedDescription = TRUE

[transform:parse_message]
FORMAT = REGEX
REGEX = ^(?<message>[^\.]+\.)

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-JSON.commit_message: TLAB-1222 More robust time parsing for Windows
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-JSON.name: Windows-JSON
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-JSON.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT=JSON

MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:Product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{Channel}}-{{EventID}}

START_TIME_FIELD = TimeCreated
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-JSON-O.name: Windows-JSON-Open Telemetry
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-JSON-O.parser_text:
[parser]
#pre-parser to conform event data keys to mapper spec and handle "data" elements
FORMAT = REGEX
REGEX = ^(?<message>.*?"event_data"\s*:\s*(?:(?<_$startofdata>\{\s*"data"\s*:\s*\[)\s*\{\s*(?<_$datadata>.*?)(?<_$endofdata>\s*\])\s*\})?.*?)$
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:_$datadata = process_data
FIND_REPLACE:message:EventData = event_data
TRANSFORM:message = parse_json
DROP:message = TRUE

[transform:process_data]
FIND_REPLACE:_$log_entry:^ = (\s*\}\s*,\s*\{\s*)
SET:formatted_data = {{_$log_entry}}
FIND_REPLACE:formatted_data: = (\s*\}\s*|\s*\{\s*)
TRANSFORM:formatted_data = parse_data
DROP:formatted_data = TRUE

[transform:parse_data]
REGEX = "(?<_$FIELD_1>[^"]*)":\s*"(?<_$VAL_1>[^\^]*)"(\^,)?
REPEAT_MATCH = TRUE
ITER_PREFIX = EventData.

[transform:parse_json]
FORMAT = JSON

#Renames to conform with mapper spec for top level fields
RENAME_FIELD:EventRecordID = record_id
RENAME_FIELD:Channel = channel
RENAME_FIELD:Task = task
RENAME_FIELD:Provider = provider
RENAME_FIELD:TimeCreated = system_time
RENAME_FIELD:Computer = computer
RENAME_FIELD:Opcode = opcode
RENAME_FIELD:Keywords = keywords
RENAME_FIELD:Details = details
RENAME_FIELD:Message = message
RENAME_FIELD:EventID = event_id
RENAME_FIELD:Level = level


#Set Mapper Info
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{Channel}}-{{event_id.id}}

#Set Time
START_TIME_FIELD = TimeCreated
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX

#Drop extraneous fields for CSE
DROP:r|details\..* = TRUE
DROP:r|EventData\.data.* = TRUE

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-NXLog.commit_message: TRIDENT-14269 Windows NXLog Parser and Shared Rename Transform
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-NXLog.name: Windows-NXLog
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-NXLog.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Field Renames = true

[parser]
# Parse Syslog Header
TRANSFORM_CASCADE = Syslog_Header_Format_01,Syslog_Header_Format_02,Syslog_No_Header

# Parse JSON
TRANSFORM:syslog_message = parse_json

# Call rename transform to add EventData. to proper fields
TRANSFORM = rename_fields

MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:Product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{Channel}}-{{EventID}}

# Align timestamp to XML
FIND_REPLACE:TimeCreated.SystemTime:T = \s

START_TIME_FIELD = TimeCreated.SystemTime
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss

DROP:syslog_message = true

[transform:Syslog_Header_Format_01]
# <14>Mar 30 13:19:24 hostname.local 
REGEX = <(?P<syslog_priority>\d{1,3})>(?P<syslog_timestamp>[a-zA-Z0-9:\s]{15})\s(?P<syslog_hostname>[a-zA-Z0-9\-_.]+)\s.*?:\s+(?P<syslog_message>.*)

[transform:Syslog_Header_Format_02]
# <36>Nov 18 08:01:02 
REGEX = <(?P<syslog_priority>\d{1,3})>(?P<syslog_timestamp>[a-zA-Z0-9:\s]{15})\s(?P<syslog_message>.*)

[transform:Syslog_No_Header]
REGEX = (?P<syslog_message>.*)

[transform:parse_json]
FORMAT = JSON

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.description: This parser is used for text based syslog windows logs.
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.name: Windows-Syslog
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Syslog Headers Microsoft = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Forwarding Headers = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Text Transforms - Security = true

[parser]
# Parse Syslog Header
TRANSFORM = Windows_Syslog_Headers

# Parse Windows Forwarding Header
TRANSFORM:_$log_entry = Parse_Windows_Text_Header

# Call the specific parser based on the event log/channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM_INDEX = win_header_channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM:Security = Security_Text_Transforms

DROP:event_log_text = true

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.commit_message: TRIDENT-14642 Update Windows Multiline parser to support Legacy CIP format
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.description: This parser is meant to be used to processed Windows event logs sent in a multi-line format that includes new line characters.
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.name: Windows-Syslog Multiline
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Field Renames = true

[parser]
#Parse syslog header if present
TRANSFORM_IF:_$log_entry:^< = parse_syslog_header

#Parse key-value pairs
TRANSFORM-kv_pairs:_$log_entry = parse_kv_pairs

#This section parses multi-value fields that are generated through the use of the ADD_VALUES flag within the parse_kv_pairs transform.
#Each of the 'split_mv_' transforms includes field names with the string '_2' appended to them; these field names are generated by the use of the SPLIT_LIST_AT_ELEMENT function and are subsequently renamed based on the standard ordering of Subject and Target usernames within Windows logs that contain multiple usernames.
#Only a select few field names are repeated within Windows event logs and only those field names are listed below
#Event logs with only one instance of these field names are routed to a transform that renames the field so that leftover list objects generated by the use of ADD_VALUES can be dropped later on in the parser without causing unintended information loss.
TRANSFORM_CASCADE-accountname = split_mv_account_name,account_name_rename
TRANSFORM_CASCADE-accountdomain = split_mv_account_domain,account_domain_rename
TRANSFORM_CASCADE-sid = split_mv_sid,sid_rename
TRANSFORM_CASCADE-logonguid = split_mv_logon_guid,logon_guid_rename
TRANSFORM_CASCADE-logonid = split_mv_logon_id,logon_id_rename

#Set CSE Configuration Information
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT-alternate_logname:Logfile = alternate_event_id_settings
MAPPER:event_id = {{LogName}}-{{EventCode}}

#Handle timestamp settings
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT-alternate_time:TimeGenerated = alternate_time_settings
#The following FIND_REPLACE operation removes the offset of -000 from events collected in Legacy CIP format
FIND_REPLACE:syslog_timestamp: = -000$
START_TIME_FIELD = syslog_timestamp
TIME_PARSER = yyyyMMddHHmmss.SSSSSS,MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss a

#Drop intermediate processing fields
TRANSFORM-drops = drop_extra_fields

#Rename fields to align with XML logging schema
TRANSFORM-renames = rename_syslog_fields
#This rename is done separately to avoid overlapping with renames performed in the shared resource
RENAME_FIELD:Computer = ComputerName

[transform:parse_syslog_header]
REGEX = <(?P<syslog_priority>\d+)>\s(?P<syslog_hostname>\S+)\s(?P<syslog_timestamp>[^\n]+)

[transform:parse_kv_pairs]
EVENT_MULTILINE = true
ADD_VALUES = TRUE
REGEX = ^\s*(?P<_$FIELD_1>[^:=<\\\n]+?)[:=][^\S\n]*"?(?P<_$VAL_1>[S\-\d%\{}\s]+}|<[a-zA-Z]>+$>|<\?xml[a-zA-Z?=".\s\d-]+>[\s\S]+|(?:\w+\n\t{3})+\w+|.*?)"?;?$
REPEAT_MATCH = true

[transform:split_mv_account_name]
SPLIT_LIST_AT_ELEMENT:Account Name = 1
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Account Name = 0
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Account Name_2 = 0
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectUserName = Account Name
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.TargetUserName = Account Name_2

[transform:split_mv_sid]
SPLIT_LIST_AT_ELEMENT:Security ID = 1
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Security ID = 0
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Security ID_2 = 0
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectUserSid = Security ID
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.TargetUserSid = Security ID_2

[transform:split_mv_account_domain]
SPLIT_LIST_AT_ELEMENT:Account Domain = 1
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Account Domain = 0
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Account Domain_2 = 0
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectDomainName = Account Domain
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.TargetDomainName = Account Domain_2

[transform:split_mv_logon_guid]
SPLIT_LIST_AT_ELEMENT:Logon GUID = 1
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Logon GUID = 0
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Logon GUID_2 = 0
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectLogonGuid = Logon GUID
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.TargetLogonGuid = Logon GUID_2

[transform:split_mv_logon_id]
SPLIT_LIST_AT_ELEMENT:Logon ID = 1
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Logon ID = 0
REPLACE_LIST_WITH_ELEMENT:Logon ID_2 = 0
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectLogonId = Logon ID
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.TargetLogonId = Logon ID_2

[transform:account_name_rename]
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectUserName = Account Name

[transform:account_domain_rename]
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectDomainName = Account Domain

[transform:sid_rename]
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.SubjectUserSid = Security ID

[transform:logon_guid_rename]
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.LogonGuid = Logon GUID

[transform:logon_id_rename]
RENAME_FIELD:EventData.LogonId = Logon ID

[transform:alternate_time_settings]
#This transform sets the value of syslog_timestamp to an alternate field if there is no syslog header attached to the log entry. If 'TimeGenerated' is not the correct field name for a given log sample, the TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT and RENAME_FIELD operations can be overwritten via local config
RENAME_FIELD:syslog_timestamp = TimeGenerated

[transform:alternate_event_id_settings]
#This transform sets the value of LogName to an alternate field if the log entry names the source log file (security, system, application) something other that 'LogName'. If 'LogFile' is not the correct field name for a given log sample, the TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT and RENAME_FIELD operations can be overwritten via local config
RENAME_FIELD:LogName = Logfile

[transform:drop_extra_fields]
DROP:event_log_text = true
DROP:kv_pairs = true
DROP:r|.* = empty
DROP:InsertionStrings = true
#The following fields are dropped as they consist of leftover list objects created by the ADD_VALUES flag being set to true within the parse_kv_pairs transform that no longer serve a purpose after being broken out.
DROP:Account Name = true
DROP:Account Domain = true
DROP:Security ID = true
DROP:Logon GUID = true
DROP:Logon ID = true

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.description: This parser is used for text based Windows logs collected via Snare.
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.name: Windows-Syslog Snare
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Syslog Headers Microsoft = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Forwarding Headers = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Field Renames = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Text Transforms - Security = true

[parser]
#Parse Syslog Header
WRAPPER = Windows_Syslog_Headers

#Parse Windows Forwarding Header
TRANSFORM:_$log_entry = Parse_Windows_Text_Header

#Parse Windows log description to reduce the remaining data to key-value pairs
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:event_log_text = parse_log_description

# Call the specific parser based on the event log/channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM_INDEX = win_header_channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM:Security = Security_Text_Transforms

#Drop intermediate processing fields
TRANSFORM-drops = drop_extra_fields

#Rename fields to align with XML logging schema
TRANSFORM-renames = rename_syslog_fields

[transform:parse_log_description]
REGEX = ^(?P<log_description>.*?)[:.](?:[^.:]*?\.)?(?:\s+(?P<kv_pairs>.*)|$)


[transform:drop_extra_fields]
DROP:event_log_text = true
DROP:kv_pairs = true
DROP:r|.* = empty
DROP:Account Name = true
DROP:Account Domain = true
DROP:Security ID = true
DROP:Logon GUID = true
DROP:Logon ID = true

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.description: This parser is used for text based Windows logs collected via Tenable LCE.
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.name: Windows-Syslog Tenable LCE
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Syslog Headers Microsoft = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Forwarding Headers = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Text Transforms - Security = true

[parser]
# Parse Syslog Header
TRANSFORM = Windows_Syslog_Headers

# Parse Windows Forwarding Header
TRANSFORM:_$log_entry = Parse_Windows_Text_Header

# Call the specific parser based on the event log/channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM_INDEX = win_header_channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM:Security = Security_Text_Transforms

DROP:event_log_text = true

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.name: Windows-Syslog WinCollect
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-Syslog.parser_text:
[dependencies]
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Syslog Headers Microsoft = true
INCLUDE:/Parsers/Sumo/Microsoft/Shared/Windows Text Transforms - Security = true

[parser]
# Parse Syslog Header
TRANSFORM = Windows_Syslog_Headers

# Parse Windows Forwarding Header
TRANSFORM:_$log_entry = Parse_Windows_Text_Header

#Parse Windows log description to reduce the remaining data to key-value pairs
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:event_log_text = parse_log_description

# Call the specific parser based on the event log/channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM_INDEX = win_header_channel
VARIABLE_TRANSFORM:Security = Security_Text_Transforms

DROP:event_log_text = true
DROP:kv_pairs = true
DROP:event_log_text = true

[transform:Parse_Windows_Text_Header]
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{win_header_channel}}-{{win_header_event_id}}
REGEX = AgentDevice\=(?P<AgentDevice>.+?\s)AgentLogFile\=(?P<win_header_channel>.+?\s)PluginVersion\=(?P<PluginVersion>.+?\s)Source\=(?P<Source>.+?\s)Computer\=(?P<Computer>.+?\s)OriginatingComputer\=(?P<OriginatingComputer>.+?\s)User\=(?P<User>.{0,60}?\s)Domain\=(?P<Domain>.{0,60}?\s)EventID\=(?P<EventID>.+?\s)EventIDCode\=(?P<win_header_event_id>.+?\s)EventType\=(?P<EventType>.+?\s)EventCategory\=(?P<EventCategory>.+?\s)RecordNumber\=(?P<RecordNumber>.+?\s)TimeGenerated\=(?P<TimeGenerated>.+?\s)TimeWritten\=(?P<TimeWritten>.+?\s)Level\=(?P<Level>.+?\s)Keywords\=(?P<Keywords>.+?\s)Task\=(?P<Task>.+?\s)Opcode\=(?P<Opcode>.+?\s)Message\=(?<event_log_text>.+)

START_TIME_FIELD = TimeGenerated
TIME_PARSER = X1, X1000

[transform:parse_log_description]
REGEX = ^(?P<log_description>.*?)[:.](?:[^.:]*?\.)?(?:\s+(?P<kv_pairs>.*)|$)

/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-XML.commit_message: TLAB-1222 More robust time parsing for Windows
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-XML.description: Parser for Windows events sent via XML regardless of originating channel (e.g. System or Security)
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-XML.name: Windows-XML
/Parsers/System/Microsoft/Windows-XML.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT = WINDOWS_XML

#Establish the mapper config globally before passing to transforms to avoid having to define a mapper for every possible EventID
MAPPER:vendor = Microsoft
MAPPER:product = Windows
MAPPER:event_id = {{Channel}}-{{EventID}}

#Handle time
TRANSFORM=Time-Parser

#Transform logic to look for file hash fields since only some sysmon logs have them
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.Hashes = parse_sysmon_hashes
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.Hash = parse_sysmon_hashes
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:EventData.ConfigurationFileHash = parse_sysmon_hashes

#Regex to handle file hash fields that are formatted as '<hash_type>=<hash_value>' and are comma separated if multiple hash types exist
[transform:parse_sysmon_hashes]
REGEX = ^(MD5=(?<md5>[^,]{32}))?,?(SHA256=(?<sha256>[^,]{64}))?,?(SHA1=(?<sha1>[^,]{40}))?,?(IMPHASH=(?<imphash>[^,]{32,64}))?
SET:file_hash_sha256 = {{sha256}}
SET:file_hash_md5 = {{md5}}
SET:file_hash_sha1 = {{sha1}}
SET:file_hash_imphash = {{imphash}}
DROP:md5 = TRUE
DROP:sha256 = TRUE
DROP:sha1 = TRUE
DROP:imphash = TRUE

[transform:Time-Parser]
START_TIME_FIELD = TimeCreated.SystemTime
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSX,yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX

/Parsers/System/Mimecast/Mimecast.commit_message: Create the new parser for the mimecast
/Parsers/System/Mimecast/Mimecast.name: Mimecast
/Parsers/System/Mimecast/Mimecast.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

# Set CSE Metadata
MAPPER:vendor = Mimecast
MAPPER:product = Mimecast
MAPPER:event_id = audit-{{category}}

TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:eventInfo = parse_eventInfo
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:messageId = set_dlp_eventID
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:reasonId = set_hold_eventID
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:search criteria = parse_json_field

# Handle the time
START_TIME_FIELD = _messagetime
TIME_PARSER = X1000

# Drop Unneccessary field 
DROP:other_msg = True
DROP:search criteria = True
DROP:kv_pair = True

[transform:parse_eventInfo]
TRANSFORM_CASCADE = parse_eventInfo_format_01, parse_eventInfo_format_02, parse_eventInfo_format_03, parse_eventInfo_format_04

[transform:parse_eventInfo_format_01]
REGEX = ^(?<action>(?i)successfully|successful|successfull|failed|fail)?([^>]*<(?<user_name>[^>]*)>,|(?i)user\s*(?<user_name>\S*)[^,]*,|[^,]*,\s*(?i)remote\s*ip\s*[^0-9]*(?<IP>%{IPV4}))\s*(?<other_msg>.*)
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:other_msg = parse_kv_pair

[transform:parse_eventInfo_format_02]
REGEX = ^(?i)remediation\s*.*(?<action>(?i)downloaded|download|created|create)[^,]*,\s*(?<other_msg>.*)
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:other_msg = parse_kv_pair

[transform:parse_eventInfo_format_03]
REGEX = ^\[(?<kv_pair>.*)\],\s*(?<other_msg>.*)
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:kv_pair = parse_kv_pair
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:other_msg = parse_kv_pair

[transform:parse_eventInfo_format_04]
REGEX = (?<msg>.*)

[transform:parse_kv_pair]
REGEX = (\s*)?(?<_$FIELD_1>[^:]*):\s*(?<_$VAL_1>\{(?:[^\{\}]|\{[^\{\}]*\})*\}|(?:\[[^\]]*\]|[^,]*))(,)?
REPEAT_MATCH = True

[transform:parse_json_field]
FORMAT = JSON

[transform:set_dlp_eventID]
MAPPER:event_id = dlp

[transform:set_hold_eventID]
MAPPER:event_id = hold-messages

/Parsers/System/Mindpoint Group/Mindpoin.commit_message: TLAB-2163: create new parser with correct vendor name
/Parsers/System/Mindpoint Group/Mindpoin.name: Mindpoint Group Keeper

/Parsers/System/Miro/Miro Audit C2C.name: Miro Audit C2C
/Parsers/System/Miro/Miro Audit C2C.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT= JSON

MAPPER:vendor = Miro
MAPPER:product = Miro Audit
MAPPER:event_id = {{event}}

#Time:
START_TIME_FIELD = createdAt
TIME_PARSER= yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ

/Parsers/System/Netskope/Netskope Securi.commit_message: TLAB-2067: updated parser to handle event id
/Parsers/System/Netskope/Netskope Securi.name: Netskope Security Cloud JSON
/Parsers/System/Netskope/Netskope Securi.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = timestamp
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSX, X1, X1000


TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:type = type_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:record_type = record_type_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:event_type = event_type_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF:audit_log_event:.*(Logout|Logoff).* = logout_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF:audit_log_event:.*(Login|Logon).* = login_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF:alert_type:DLP = dlp_alert_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF:alert_name:Bulk Download = bulk_download_eventid
TRANSFORM_IF:alert_name:^\s*$ = set_alert_name

#Set mapper information
MAPPER:vendor = Netskope
MAPPER:product = Security Cloud
MAPPER:event_id = {{event_name}}

[transform:parse_message]
FORMAT = JSON

[transform:type_eventid]
SET:event_name = {{type}}

[transform:record_type_eventid]
SET:event_name = {{record_type}}

[transform:event_type_eventid]
SET:event_name = {{event_type}}

[transform:logout_eventid]
SET:event_name = audit-logout

[transform:login_eventid]
SET:event_name = audit-logon

[transform:dlp_alert_eventid]
SET:event_name = dlp-alert

[transform:bulk_download_eventid]
SET:event_name = bulk_download

[transform:set_alert_name]
SET:alert_name = netskope_alert

/Parsers/System/Netskope/Netskope WebTx.name: Netskope WebTx
/Parsers/System/Netskope/Netskope WebTx.parser_text:
[parser]
TRANSFORM_IF_ELSE:_$log_entry:\{.*\} = parse_json_log,parse_csv_log

# CSE Meta data
MAPPER:vendor = Netskope
MAPPER:product = WebTx
MAPPER:event_id = webtx

SET:timestamp = {{date}} {{time}}

# Handle the timestamp
START_TIME_FIELD = timestamp
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss, dd/MM/yy HH:mm:ss

[transform:parse_json_log]
FORMAT = JSON

[transform:parse_csv_log]
TRANSFORM_IF_ELSE:_$log_entry:^\d+-\d{2}-\d{2} = csv_format_01,csv_format_02

on,x-sr-ssl-handshake-error,x-sr-ssl-ja3s,x-sr-ssl-malformed-ssl,x-sr-ssl-version,x-ssl-bypass,x-ssl-bypass-reason,x-ssl-policy-action,x-ssl-policy-categories,x-ssl-policy-dst-host,x-ssl-policy-dst-host-source,x-ssl-policy-dst-ip,x-ssl-policy-name,x-ssl-policy-src-ip,x-transaction-id,x-type,x-cs-app-instance-name,x-cs-app-instance-tag,x-cs-app-object-type
DROP:c-ip=-
DROP:x-cs-src-ip=-
DROP:x-cs-userip=-

[transform:csv_format_02]
FORMAT = CSV
EVENT_MULTILINE = False
FIELD_DELIMS=","
FIELDS = bytes,c-ip,cs-bytes,cs-content-type,cs-dns,cs-host,cs-method,cs-referer,cs-uri,cs-uri-port,cs-uri-query,cs-uri-scheme,cs-user-agent,cs-username,date,rs-status,s-ip,sc-bytes,sc-content-type,sc-status,time,time-taken,x-c-browser,x-c-browser-version,x-c-country,x-c-device,x-c-latitude,x-c-local-time,x-c-location,x-c-longitude,x-c-os,x-c-region,x-c-zipcode,x-category,x-category-id,x-client-ssl-err,x-cs-access-method,x-cs-app,x-cs-app-activity,x-cs-app-category,x-cs-app-cci,x-cs-app-ccl,x-cs-app-from-user,x-cs-app-instance-id,x-cs-app-instance-name,x-cs-app-instance-tag,x-cs-app-object-id,x-cs-app-object-name,x-cs-app-object-type,x-cs-app-suite,x-cs-app-tags,x-cs-app-to-user,x-cs-connect-host,x-cs-connect-port,x-cs-connect-user-agent,x-cs-domain-fronted-sni,x-cs-dst-ip,x-cs-dst-port,x-cs-http-version,x-cs-ip-connect-xff,x-cs-ip-xff,x-cs-page-id,x-cs-session-id,x-cs-site,x-cs-sni,x-cs-src-ip,x-cs-src-ip-egress,x-cs-src-port,x-cs-ssl-cipher,x-cs-ssl-engine-action,x-cs-ssl-engine-action-reason,x-cs-ssl-fronting-error,x-cs-ssl-handshake-error,x-cs-ssl-ja3,x-cs-ssl-version,x-cs-timestamp,x-cs-traffic-type,x-cs-tunnel-id,x-cs-uri-path,x-cs-url,x-cs-userip,x-error,x-other-category,x-other-category-id,x-policy-action,x-policy-dst-host,x-policy-dst-host-source,x-policy-dst-ip,x-policy-justification-reason,x-policy-justification-type,x-policy-name,x-policy-src-ip,x-r-cert-enddate,x-r-cert-expired,x-r-cert-incomplete-chain,x-r-cert-issuer-cn,x-r-cert-mismatch,x-r-cert-revocation-check,x-r-cert-revoked,x-r-cert-self-signed,x-r-cert-startdate,x-r-cert-subject-cn,x-r-cert-untrusted-root,x-r-cert-valid,x-request-id,x-rs-file-category,x-rs-file-language,x-rs-file-md5,x-rs-file-sha256,x-rs-file-size,x-rs-file-type,x-s-country,x-s-custom-signing-ca-error,x-s-dp-name,x-s-latitude,x-s-location,x-s-longitude,x-s-region,x-s-zipcode,x-sc-notification-name,x-server-ssl-err,x-sr-dst-ip,x-sr-dst-port,x-sr-headers-name,x-sr-headers-value,x-sr-src-ip,x-sr-src-port,x-sr-ssl-cipher,x-sr-ssl-client-certificate-error,x-sr-ssl-engine-action,x-sr-ssl-engine-action-reason,x-sr-ssl-handshake-error,x-sr-ssl-ja3s,x-sr-ssl-malformed-ssl,x-sr-ssl-version,x-ssl-bypass,x-ssl-bypass-reason,x-ssl-policy-action,x-ssl-policy-categories,x-ssl-policy-dst-host,x-ssl-policy-dst-host-source,x-ssl-policy-dst-ip,x-ssl-policy-name,x-ssl-policy-src-ip,x-transaction-id,x-type
DROP:c-ip=-
DROP:x-cs-src-ip=-
DROP:x-cs-userip=-

/Parsers/System/Nginx/Nginx Syslog.description: Parser for Nginx access logs sent via syslog in the default "combined" format.
/Parsers/System/Nginx/Nginx Syslog.name: Nginx Syslog
/Parsers/System/Nginx/Nginx Syslog.parser_text:
###################################
#            Resources            #
###################################
#https://docs.nginx.com/nginx/admin-guide/monitoring/logging/

[parser]
FORMAT = REGEX

#The following regex assumes that the Nginx access logs being parsed are using the default "combined" format defined in '/etc/nginx/nginx.conf'. Should a format other than the default be in use, this REGEX parameter will need to be overwritten via local configuration to accommodate.
REGEX = ^(?<remote_addr>\S+) - (?<remote_user>.*?) \[(?<timestamp>[^\]]+)\] ("(?<http_fqdn>[^"]*)" )?"(?<http_request>(?<http_method>[A-Z]+)\s(?<http_uri>.*?)\s(?<http_version>HTTP\/[\d.]+))" (?<http_status>\d+) (?<body_bytes_sent>\d+) "(?<http_referer>[^"]+)" "(?<http_user_agent>[^"]+)"(?:\s"(?<x_forwarded_for>[^"]+)")?

MAPPER:vendor = Nginx
MAPPER:product = Nginx
MAPPER:event_id = access_log

#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = timestamp
TIME_PARSER = dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss Z

/Parsers/System/OCSF/OCSF.description: Supports Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework Version 1.5 developed for Amazon Security Lake and AWS Security Hub with limited backward compatibility to earlier schema versions.
/Parsers/System/OCSF/OCSF.name: OCSF
/Parsers/System/OCSF/OCSF.parser_text:
[parser]
TRANSFORM_IF_ELSE:_$log_entry:^.*?"detail"\s*:\s*\{[^\}]+\} = parse_wrapper, parse_json

# Metadata
MAPPER:vendor = {{metadata.product.vendor_name}}
MAPPER:product = {{metadata.product.name}} - OCSF
MAPPER:event_id = {{category_name}}.{{class_name}}.{{activity_name}}

TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:resources.array.details = format_resource_details

# Time Parsing
START_TIME_FIELD = time
TIME_PARSER = X1000

# Format the observables
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^observables\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.type:r|^observables\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.value = parse_observable_
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^observables\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.type_id:r|^observables\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.value = parse_observable_
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^observables\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.name:r|^observables\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.value = parse_observable_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:observables.type:observables.value = parse_observable_
ZIP_NO_DROP:observables.type_id:observables.value = parse_observable_
ZIP_NO_DROP:observables.name:observables.value = parse_observable_

# Format the enrichments	
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^enrichments\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.name:r|^enrichments\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+|array)\.value = enrichment_

# Format the file events
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^evidences\.1\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^evidences\.1\.file.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^file_result\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^file_result\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>driver)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>driver)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>process)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>process)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>module)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>module)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>job)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>job)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>script)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>script)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>query_evidence)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>query_evidence)\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^(?<type>osint)\.1\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.algorithm_id:r|^(?<type>osint)\.1\.file\.hashes\.(?<_$INDEX>\d+)\.value = file_hash_{{type}}_ 

# Format the resources
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^resources\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.type:r|^resources\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.uid$ = parseResourceUid.{{_$count}}.
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^resources\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.type:r|^resources\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.uid_alt = parseResourceUidAlt.{{_$count}}.
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^resources\.array\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.type:r|^resources\.array\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.uid$ = parseResourceUid.{{_$count}}.
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^resources\.array\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.type:r|^resources\.array\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.uid_alt = parseResourceUidAlt.{{_$count}}.
ZIP_NO_DROP:resources.type:resources.uid = parseResourceUid.
ZIP_NO_DROP:resources.type:resources.uid_alt = parseResourceUidAlt.
ZIP_NO_DROP:resources.array.type:resources.array.uid = parseResourceUid.
ZIP_NO_DROP:resources.array.type:resources.array.uid_alt = parseResourceUidAlt.

ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^resources\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.type:r|^resources\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.name = parseResourceName.{{_$count}}.
ZIP_NO_DROP:r|^resources\.array\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.type:r|^resources\.array\.(?<_$INDEX>(?<_$count>\d+))\.name = parseResourceName.{{_$count}}.
ZIP_NO_DROP:resources.type:resources.name = parseResourceName.
ZIP_NO_DROP:resources.array.type:resources.array.name = parseResourceName.

[transform:parse_wrapper]
REGEX = ^.*?detail":\s*\{\s*"[^"]+":\s*\[\s*(?<_$ocsf_log>\{.*\})\s*\][\s*\}]+$
TRANSFORM:_$ocsf_log = parse_json

[transform:format_resource_details]
REGEX = (?<resource>[^=]*)=\s*(?<resource_obj>\{(?:[^\{\}]|\{[^\{\}]*\})*\})
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:resource_obj = parse_json
DROP:resource_obj = True
DROP:resources.array.details = True

[transform:parse_json]
FORMAT = JSON

/Parsers/System/OSSEC/OSSEC JSON.description: Parser for OSSEC alerts in JSON format, as seen in the alerts.json log file
/Parsers/System/OSSEC/OSSEC JSON.name: OSSEC JSON
/Parsers/System/OSSEC/OSSEC JSON.parser_text:
###################################
#            Resources            #
###################################
#https://www.ossec.net/docs/manual/output/json-alert-log-output.html
#Note: The above documentation describes how to output OSSEC alerts in JSON format. This is preferred as custom log formats may not be globally parsable, but formatted data such as JSON will virtually always be parsable and usually can also be mapped to a CSE record.

[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

#Set mapping information
MAPPER:vendor = OSSEC
MAPPER:product = OSSEC
MAPPER:event_id = alert

#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = TimeStamp
TIME_PARSER = X1000

#The following IF statement will send the "full_log" field to the standard Linux parser if the contained log message came from a Linux audit log for a supported process. You can learn more about our Linux support at the following link:
#https://help.sumologic.com/Cloud_SIEM_Enterprise/CSE_Ingestion/Linux_OS_Syslog
#Please note that this transform will change the Vendor, Product, and Event ID associated with each log message to use our standard Linux mappers for normalization and detection purposes
TRANSFORM_IF:full_log:.*(?:crontab|crond|cron|CRON|CROND|gpasswd|groupadd|groupdel|groupmod|passwd|sshd|su|sudo|useradd|userdel|usermod|adclient|systemd|omiserver|dhclient|systemdjournal|fw|kernel|pkexec)[\[:] = parse_linux_message

[transform:parse_linux_message]
#The following operation parses embedded log messages using the global Linux parser
PARSE = /Parsers/Sumo/Linux/Linux OS Syslog

#Set a static field called "original_log_source" to identify Linux logs that originated from OSSEC
#This field can be searched via the "fields" field to separate OSSEC collection from native Linux collection
SET:original_log_source = OSSEC

#Drop redundant fields
DROP:syslog_message = true

/Parsers/System/Okta/Okta.description: Parser for Okta logs in native JSON format
/Parsers/System/Okta/Okta.name: Okta
/Parsers/System/Okta/Okta.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:detail.eventType = parse_format1
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:eventType = parse_fromat2

# Set CSE Metadata
MAPPER:vendor = Okta
MAPPER:product = SSO
MAPPER:event_id = {{event_type}}

#Remove unneeded index numbers where applicable
JSON_FLATTEN_SINGLE_LISTS = true

#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = _messagetime
TIME_PARSER = X1000

# extract user email 
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:actor.alternateId = extract_user_email
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:detail.actor.alternateId = extract_user_email
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:detail.target.1.alternateId = extract_target_email
TRANSFORM_FIELD_IF_PRESENT:detail.target.2.alternateId = extract_target_email

#Re-name fields
RENAME_FIELD:displayMessage = detail.displayMessage
RENAME_FIELD:outcome.reason = detail.outcome.reason
RENAME_FIELD:outcome.result = detail.outcome.result

#Call transform to parse 1-2 usernames out of the record. This is done because Okta often logs username strings in arbitrary nested locations in the JSON based on other data in the record
TRANSFORM_IF:_$log_entry:^.*?Id":"(?P<user>[\w\.$-]+?@\w[^.]+?\.[a-z]{1,3})(?:"|\.)(.+Id":"(?P<target_user>[\w.$-]+?@[^.]+?\.[a-z]{1,3})(?:"|\.))? = parse_usernames

#Drop user when the user is system@okta.com
DROP:user = system@okta.com
DROP:actor.alternateId = system@okta.com
DROP:user_email = system@okta.com
DROP:actor.displayName = Okta System
DROP:detail.actor.displayName = Okta System
DROP:detail.user = Okta System
DROP:detail.user = system@okta.com
DROP:detail.actor.alternateId = system@okta.com

#Regex will parse 1 or 2 usernames out of the log, does not require that 2 be present
[transform:parse_usernames]
REGEX = ^.*?Id":"(?P<user>[\w\.$-]+?@\w[^.]+?\.[a-z]{1,3}(?:\.[a-z]{1,3})?)(?:"|\.)(.+Id":"(?P<target_user>[\w.$-]+?@[^.]+?\.[a-z]{1,3}(?:\.[a-z]{1,3})?)(?:"|\.))?

[transform:parse_format1]
SET:event_type = {{detail.eventType}}

[transform:parse_fromat2]
SET:event_type = {{eventType}}

[transform:extract_user_email]
REGEX = ((?<user_email>^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$)|(?<actor_alternate>\S+))

[transform:extract_target_email]
REGEX = ((?<target_email>^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$)|(?<target_alternate>\S+))

/Parsers/System/OneLogin/OneLogin SSO JS.commit_message: TRIDENT-12699 Create OneLogin SSO JSON custom parser
/Parsers/System/OneLogin/OneLogin SSO JS.description: Parser for OneLogin SSO logs sent in JSON format
/Parsers/System/OneLogin/OneLogin SSO JS.name: OneLogin SSO JSON
/Parsers/System/OneLogin/OneLogin SSO JS.parser_text:
###################################
#            Resources            #
###################################
#https://developers.onelogin.com/api-docs/1/events/event-resource

[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

#Handle time and allow for multiple timestamp field names to exist
TRANSFORM_IF_NOT_PRESENT-timestamp:event.event_timestamp = set_alternate_timestamp
START_TIME_FIELD = event.event_timestamp
TIME_PARSER = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z

#Set mapper information and allow for multiple event_id field names to exist
MAPPER:vendor = OneLogin
MAPPER:product = SSO
TRANSFORM_IF_NOT_PRESENT-event_id:event.event_type_id = set_alternate_event_id
MAPPER:event_id = {{event.event_type_id}}

[transform:set_alternate_timestamp]
RENAME_FIELD:event.event_timestamp = fields.event.event_timestamp

[transform:set_alternate_event_id]
RENAME_FIELD:event.event_type_id = fields.event.event_type_id

/Parsers/System/OpenAI/OpenAI Audit.name: OpenAI Audit

MAPPER:vendor = OpenAI

START_TIME_FIELD = effective_at

/Parsers/System/OpenVPN/OpenVPN Syslog.commit_message: TLAB-1847:Updated the Parser to support customer logs
/Parsers/System/OpenVPN/OpenVPN Syslog.description: Parser for OpenVPN logs sent in standard format via syslog
/Parsers/System/OpenVPN/OpenVPN Syslog.name: OpenVPN Syslog
/Parsers/System/OpenVPN/OpenVPN Syslog.parser_text:
###################################
#            Resources            #
###################################
#https://openvpn.net/vpn-server-resources/logging-and-debug-flag-options-for-access-server/

[parser]
FORMAT = REGEX

#Determine format for transform routing
TRANSFORM-format = parse_format
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:format1 = parse_format1
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:format2 = parse_format2
TRANSFORM_IF_PRESENT:format3 = parse_format3

#Set mapping information
MAPPER:vendor = OpenVPN
MAPPER:product = OpenVPN
MAPPER:event_id = {{message_type}}


#Handle time
START_TIME_FIELD = _messagetime
TIME_PARSER = X1000


#Parse log header
[transform:parse_format1]
TRANSFORM-header = parse_openvpn_header

#Pass log message to a transform cascade for parsing
TRANSFORM:log_message = parse_openvpn_message

[transform:parse_format]
REGEX = ((?<format1>\w{3}\s\w{3}\s\d{1,2}\s\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\s\d{4}\s(?:[^/]+/)?[^:\s]+:\d+\s.*)|(?<format2>^.*?\[stdout#\w+\].*)|(?<format3><\d+>\s*\S+\s(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2})\s)\s*(.*))

[transform:parse_openvpn_header]
REGEX = .*\w{3}\s(?P<timestamp>\w{3}\s\d{1,2}\s\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\s\d{4})\s(?:(?P<username>[^/]+)/)?(?P<src_ip>[^:\s]+):(?P<src_port>\d+)\s(?P<log_message>.*)

[transform:parse_format_3_header]
REGEX = ((?:<\d+>)\s*(?<syslog_hostname>\S*)\s(?<syslog_timestamp>\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}))\s(?<log_message>.*)

[transform:parse_openvpn_message]
TRANSFORM_CASCADE = parse_user_auth_success,parse_user_auth_failure,parse_multi_learn,parse_multi_primary,parse_ptp_init,parse_format_3_1,network_3_2,network_4,implicit_log_drop

[transform:parse_user_auth_success]
REGEX = ^(?P<message_type>[^:]+):\sUsername/Password\sauthentication\s(?P<action>\S+)\sfor\susername\s'(?P<username>[^']+)'\s*\[(?<flags>[^\]]+)\]

[transform:parse_user_auth_failure]
REGEX = ^(?P<message_type>[^:]+):\sAuth\sUsername/Password\sverification\s(?P<action>\S+)\sfor\speer

[transform:parse_multi_learn]
REGEX = ^(?P<message_type>[^:]+):\s(?P<action>[^:]+):\s(?P<assigned_ip>\S+)\s->

[transform:parse_multi_primary]
REGEX = ^(?P<message_type>[^:]+):\s.*:\s(?P<assigned_ip>\S+)$

[transform:parse_ptp_init]
REGEX = ^\[(?P<username>[^\]]+)\]\s(?P<message_type>Peer\sConnection)\s(?P<action>\S+)\swith\s\[(?P<address_family>[^\]]+)\]

[transform:implicit_log_drop]
DROP = true

[transform:parse_format2]
TRANSFORM_CASCADE = auth_failure,network_1,network_2,auth_success,implicit_log_drop
DROP:log = true

[transform:auth_success]
REGEX = ^(?P<timestamp>\S+[-+]\d{4})\s+\[\S+\]\s+(?P<message_type>AUTH SUCCESS)\s+(?P<log>.*)
TRANSFORM:log = parse_log

[transform:parse_log]
REGEX = (?P<_$FIELD_1>[^'={},]+)':\s+'?(?P<_$VAL_1>[^'=}{,]+)
REPEAT_MATCH = true

[transform:auth_failure]
REGEX = ^(?P<timestamp>\S+?[+-]\d{4})\s+\[\S+\]\s+.*?:\s+"(?P<time>\d+\-\d+\-\d+\s+\d+\:\d+\:\d+)\s+(?P<src_ip>\S+?)\:(?P<src_port>\d+)\s+(?P<message>.*?)\s\[(?P<user>.*?)\]:\s+'(?P<message_type>\S+?)(?:'|,)(?:\S+(?P<reason>.*?)')?\s+\(status=(?P<status>\d+)

[transform:network_1]
REGEX = ^(?P<timestamp>\S+?[+-]\d{4})\s+\[\S+\]\s+.*?:\s+'(?P<time>\d+\-\d+\-\d+\s+\d+\:\d+\:\d+)\s+(?P<user>\S+?)\/(?P<src_ip>\S+?)\:(?P<src_port>\d+)\s+(?P<host>\w+)\[(?P<action>.*?)\]\s+(?P<status>\S+?),\s+(?P<event>.*?)'
SET:message_type = network

[transform:network_2]
REGEX = ^(?P<timestamp>\S+?[+-]\d{4})\s+\[\S+\]\s+.*?:\s+'(?P<time>\d+\-\d+\-\d+\s+\d+\:\d+\:\d+)\s+(?P<src_ip>\S+?)\:(?P<src_port>\d+)\s+\[(?P<user>\S+)\]\s+(?P<event>.*?)'
SET:message_type = network

[transform:parse_format3]
TRANSFORM-header = parse_format_3_header

#Pass log message to a transform cascade for parsing
TRANSFORM:log_message = parse_openvpn_message

DROP:format3 = True

[transform:parse_format_3_1]
REGEX = ^(?<src_ip>(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})(\.(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})){3}):(?<src_port>\d+)(?:[\s\S]*)(?<message_type>Peer\sConnection)\s(?<action>Initiated)(?:[\s\S]*):(?<dst_ip>(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})(\.(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})){3}):(?<dst_port>\d+)

[transform:network_3_2]
REGEX = ^(?<protocol>TCP)\s(?<action>(connection\sestablished))[\s\S]*:(?<src_i>(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})(\.(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})){3}):(?<src_port>\d+)
SET:message_type = network

[transform:network_4]
REGEX =  (?<src_ip>(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})(\.(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|1\d{2}|\d{1,2})){3}):(?<src_port>\d+)(?:[\s\S]*)(?<action>Connection\sreset|connection-reset)
SET:message_type = network

[transform:implicit_log_drop]
DROP = true

/Parsers/System/Oracle/Oracle Cloud Infr.commit_message: TLAB-2216: updated the parser to support auth event
/Parsers/System/Oracle/Oracle Cloud Infr.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
/Parsers/System/Oracle/Oracle Cloud Infr.parser_text:
[parser]
FORMAT = JSON

#Set mapper information
MAPPER:vendor = Oracle
MAPPER:product = Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
MAPPER:event_id = audit-{{eventName}}

#handle time 
START_TIME_FIELD = _messagetime
TIME_PARSER = X1000

TRANSFORM_IF:message:\[[^\]]* = parse_msg
DROP:message = \[[^\]]*

[transform:parse_msg]
REGEX = \[(?<syslog_timestamp>[^\]]*)\]\s*[^\]]*\]\s*\[pid\s*(?<pid>\d+):tid\s*(?<tid>\d+)\]\s*(?<action>Login|Logging\s*out)\s*(?:(?<result>successful|failed)\s*for)?\s*user\s*"(?:(?<user_email>[^@]+@[^\.]+\.[a-zA-Z]+)|(?<user_name>%{USERNAME}))"(,\s*remote\s*address\s*(?<src_ip>%{IP}))?
MAPPER:event_id = auth-{{action}}

/Parsers/System/Orca Security/Orca Secur.name: Orca Security

#Setting footprints to mapper

#Formulate Entity Identifier
ZIP_NO_DROP:asset_type:asset_name = %s_asset_name

#Handle time

/Parsers/System/Osquery/Osquery JSON.description: Parser for Osquery events sent in JSON format

/Parsers/System/Osquery/Osquery JSON.parser_text:

#https://osquery.readthedocs.io/en/latest/deployment/logging/
